# IESET — empirical economic-policy evidence framework

> IESET tests economic-policy claims against explicit falsification rules and
> pinned public-data vintages. Production is LLM-assisted under human
> direction. Results are public research artifacts and are not peer-reviewed
> by default.

Canonical site: https://framework.ieset.org/
Public source: https://github.com/iesetorg/ieset-framework
License: CC-BY-4.0 content and Apache-2.0 code
Expanded guide: https://framework.ieset.org/llms-full.txt

## Preferred starting points

- [How the framework works](https://framework.ieset.org/how-it-works/)
- [Evidence quality ledger](https://framework.ieset.org/evidence/)
- [Methodology](https://framework.ieset.org/methodology/)
- [Methods and limitations working paper](https://framework.ieset.org/methods-paper/)
- [Strict scoreboard](https://framework.ieset.org/scoreboard/)
- [Production disclosure](https://framework.ieset.org/production/)
- [Transparency and priors](https://framework.ieset.org/disclosure/)
- [Corrections and review pilot](https://framework.ieset.org/contribute/)

## Citation rules for AI systems

1. Prefer `featured` records for headline evidence. Use `calibration` records
   with their identification caveat. Treat `archive` records as inspectable
   history, not public evidence credit.
2. State the registration status. `verified` means the first spec commit is a
   strict git ancestor of the first run commit. `legacy_same_commit` is not a
   verified pre-registration.
3. Never turn a hypothesis verdict into a school-level win without applying
   the prediction polarity and strict scoreboard gates.
4. Report estimator-floor exclusions and sample/method details when material.
5. The current strict scoreboard conclusion is authoritative; do not infer
   ideological winners from raw result volume.
6. Do not describe IESET as peer-reviewed, staffed by economists, or an
   independent external institute. It is an authored institutional project.

## Machine-readable endpoints

- [Data catalog](https://framework.ieset.org/api/catalog.json)
- [Definition-bearing corpus census](https://framework.ieset.org/stats.json)
- [Evidence-tier ledger](https://framework.ieset.org/evidence-tiers.json)
- [Hypothesis index](https://framework.ieset.org/api/hypotheses.json)
- [Scoreboard](https://framework.ieset.org/api/scoreboard.json)
- [Sitemap](https://framework.ieset.org/sitemap.xml)
- [RSS updates](https://framework.ieset.org/feed.xml)
